name: Crisp Pickled Watermelon Rind
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and remove all the green and pink portions from the rind of a large watermelon. Cut the rind into cubes or slices.
2. Measure 4 quarts of rind.
3. Drop the rind into a kettle of boiling water, boil for 5 minutes, and drain.
4. Cool.
5. Dissolve 4 tablespoons slaked lime (obtainable at your drugstore) in 2 quarts cold water.
6. Pour the solution over the watermelon rind and let it stand for 3 hours.
7. Drain and rinse thoroughly.
8. Cover the rind with clear cold water, bring to a boil, and boil until the rind is tender.
9. Combine 2 cups sugar, 1 cup vinegar, and 4 cups water in a separate container.
10. Add 2 tablespoons each of whole allspice and whole cloves, 4 sticks cinnamon, and 2 pieces ginger root (all tied in a bag) to the boiling syrup.
11. Bring to a boil and boil for 5 minutes.
12. Add the rind and 2 hot peppers; bring back to a boil and simmer for 30 minutes.
13. Let the rind stand in the syrup in a cool place for 12 to 24 hours.
14. Adjust sweetness by adding 3 cups vinegar and 2-4 cups sugar according to taste, then bring to a boil and simmer until the rind is transparent.
15. If the syrup becomes too thick before the rind is clear, add 1/2 cup hot water from time to time as needed.
16. Discard the spice bag.
17. Pack the rind and boiling-hot syrup into hot jars and seal.
This recipe makes 8 pints.
